reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 3 cfs @ Fort Collins , 1 cfs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir We are starting to see air temperatures start to decline and more early spring like weather! Flows have began to level out a bit, and water clarity is improving. Looks like an early, light terrestrial bite is starting to show up. Consider running a dry-dropper rig in the afternoons, as there are plenty of ants and beetles moving around. With spring moving in on the Poudre, bug diversity is improving. Larger Stonefly and caddis imitations have been finding success. With the off-color water, attractor patterns and brighter flies have been producing as well. As the water begins to settle more, you can move more towards your smaller Blue Wing, Stonefly, and caddis imitations. Spring is definitely starting to show its face around here, and we’re getting more guide trips out on the Poudre. Give us a call to get the lowdown! Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year.
